signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7051 anchors the week's dominant G10 theme as the USD leader-to-AUD laggard spread hits 1.295%. Despite a 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), AUD is underperforming due to a negative CESI (net -9 misses) and extreme COT positioning (LF net long +56,800, 76.5% long). USD strength is reinforced by a bear steepener regime (2s10s +86bp) and a positive CESI (+31 beats). The divergence between high AUD carry and weak price action signals a structural trend shift rather than a temporary dip.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.21 trades above the critical 160.00 threshold, increasing the risk of BoJ intervention. JPY COT LF net short -105,136 remains crowded, while the options market is pricing a moderate put skew (25d RR at -1.10) as a hedge against JPY strength. Elevated vol premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%, VRP +3.9%) indicates institutional demand for protection despite the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry bid. Carry remains the primary driver until VIX crosses 25, but the break of 160.00 shifts the regime toward intervention-watch.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Oil Shock
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3973 tests new 2026 highs as WTI crude prices drop -3.32% to $84.8 on Iran peace headlines. A significant correlation break (z = -1.57σ) vs oil suggests the pair is overextending relative to energy fundamentals. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 91% short, diverging from the COT net short CAD position (-49,052). This institutional vs retail divergence at multi-year highs creates a high-velocity reversal risk if oil stabilizes or retail capitulates. Watch for a failure to hold 1.4000 to trigger a squeeze.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Macro + Vol Complacency
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3416 remains under pressure following weak UK GDP data and a neutral CESI (+3). While the Fed-BoE carry is neutral (0bp), the options market is pricing an extreme vol premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0%, VRP +5.0%), the highest among G10 majors excluding AUD. This suggests the market is paying a significant premium for downside protection (25d RR -0.71) ahead of the June 18 BoE meeting. GBP COT LF net long +27,022 (65.6%) is vulnerable to further liquidation if BoE signals a dovish shift relative to the Fed.
